name: Second-Order Thinking
what_it_is: Asking not just "what happens next?" (first-order) but "what happens after that?" and "what happens after that?"
core_mechanic: Write the first-order consequence. Ask what that causes. Ask what that causes. Second and third-order effects are where surprises live - both bad ones you didn't anticipate and good ones you didn't see.
when_to_use: Any decision with significant consequences. Especially when an option looks obviously good at first-order and nobody is questioning it.
when_not_to_use: When failure modes are already obvious and the forward analysis is underdeveloped.
ideaos_application: Agent 3 runs second and third-order analysis on the recommended direction. Specifically looks for second-order effects that contradict the first-order benefit.
